content: Combining the task-agnostic iterative self-feedback framework of Self-Refine with the multiple inconsistent perspective design of modern LLM self-reflection mechanisms can yield a new lightweight reflection approach where Self-Refine's original single-perspective iterative refinement is augmented by sampling inconsistent problem-solving perspectives at each iteration to generate more diverse self-feedback, without requiring any additional training or external supervision. This approach fixes the key limitation of vanilla Self-Refine where repeated refinement can get stuck in local blind spots caused by the model's consistent inherent bias.
